rubric_cards.claim: The plan’s load-bearing repo-state claims are anchored to the current artifact and to directly inspected workflow, CLI, operations, API, bridge, and test files.
rubric_cards.evidence: Verified against packages/workflows/src/executor.ts, packages/workflows/src/dag-executor.ts, packages/workflows/src/schemas/workflow-run.ts, packages/workflows/src/schemas/workflow.ts, packages/cli/src/commands/workflow.ts, packages/core/src/operations/workflow-operations.ts, packages/server/src/routes/api.ts, packages/server/src/adapters/web/workflow-bridge.ts, packages/cli/src/commands/workflow.test.ts, and packages/server/src/routes/api.workflow-runs.test.ts.

rubric_cards.claim: Artifact evidence is strong; conversation and campaign evidence were used only in a scoped way.

prior_round_closure.title: Declared in-scope workflow run --json and workflow resume --json surfaces lack end-gate proof
prior_round_closure.caller_claim: The plan previously lacked final-gate proof for run/resume JSON surfaces.
prior_round_closure.verification_notes: Current artifact now adds explicit P99 CLI verify commands, evidence, and exit criteria for both workflow run --json and workflow resume --json, and those surfaces align with the currently inspected CLI/runtime split.

review.closure_verification: Both carried issues are resolved in the current artifact. No remaining freeze blocker was verified in the inspected slice.
workflow_compliance: Plan remains plan-only and does not drift into implementation steps.
Phase structure includes explicit exit criteria across freeze, runtime, CLI, parity, and repo-wide validation gates.
Current artifact now includes explicit end-gate proof for run/resume JSON surfaces.
The plan correctly distinguishes durable read-back, mutating JSON envelopes, and v1 API raw-read-back parity boundaries.
technical_reality: Current executor/schema surface still collapses paused execution to { success: true, paused: true, workflowRunId } in packages/workflows/src/executor.ts and packages/workflows/src/schemas/workflow.ts.
Current CLI run/resume/approve/reject surfaces are prose-first and do not currently expose JSON contracts; only list/status currently expose json flags in packages/cli/src/commands/workflow.ts.
Current operations layer exposes status/resume/approve/reject/abandon behavior but no durable per-run show/read-back contract in packages/core/src/operations/workflow-operations.ts.
Current API already exposes raw GET /api/workflows/runs/:runId and inline approve/reject logic in packages/server/src/routes/api.ts.
Current web bridge already emits a paused approval-shaped SSE event in packages/server/src/adapters/web/workflow-bridge.ts.
The cited CLI/API test homes are real and already adjacent to the behaviors the plan intends to extend.
assumptions_to_verify: Adding gate token/version to persisted approval metadata can be done compatibly for legacy paused runs.
A single snapshot builder can be shared or kept behaviorally identical across CLI and API surfaces without introducing drift.
JSON-mode suppression can be wired so stdout stays parse-clean while stderr remains available for human diagnostics.
verification_sufficiency: Sufficient to judge the current artifact’s compliance with the two carried issues and its main load-bearing repo-grounding claims.
Not sufficient to claim full-thread or full-campaign coverage.
Because no commands were executed, this review verifies plan grounding and test-home realism, not runtime pass/fail.
downstream_risks: Implementation still spans workflow runtime, CLI, operations, and API; drift risk remains if snapshot building and stale-gate validation are not centralized or kept strictly equivalent.
Legacy paused-run compatibility is planned clearly, but correctness still depends on the future tests actually being added and passing.
JSON parse-safety will depend on suppressing existing prose output paths during new json-mode flows.
review.freeze_recommendation: Safe to freeze. No blocker-level issues were identified.
